name: Leptogenesis
summary: Heavy right-handed neutrinos decay out of equilibrium with CP-violating asymmetries, generating a lepton asymmetry that is partially converted to a baryon asymmetry by SM electroweak sphalerons. Builds directly on the type-I seesaw: the same heavy Majorana sector that explains light neutrino masses also generates BAU.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces eta ~ 10^-9 with M_R > 10^9 GeV (Davidson-Ibarra bound) for hierarchical heavy neutrinos. Resonant leptogenesis lowers the mass scale at the cost of fine-tuning.
Requires the seesaw structure to exist; this edge tracks the dependency.

touches_benchmarks.note: High-scale CP violation does not generically pollute low-energy EDMs at observable levels.

introduces.note: Building on seesaw, leptogenesis introduces no new fields. The asymmetry depends on CP phases in the heavy-neutrino Yukawa matrix; counting them as ~2 new effectively-free parameters captures the additional information needed.

history: Fukugita and Yanagida (1986). Davidson-Ibarra bound on lightest M_R (2002). Buchmuller-Di Bari-Plumacher boltzmann analysis through the 2000s. Resonant leptogenesis (Pilaftsis-Underwood) lowers the scale.

references.citation: Fukugita and Yanagida, 'Baryogenesis without grand unification', Phys. Lett. B 174, 45 (1986)
Davidson and Ibarra, 'A lower bound on the right-handed neutrino mass from leptogenesis', Phys. Lett. B 535, 25 (2002)
